evidence: plugin.json has no 'commands' key; commands/setup.md is present on disk at commands/setup.md
description: plugin.json omits the commands array; commands/setup.md exists on disk and would not be registered with the Claude Code plugin system

evidence: frontmatter contains only 'description:'; no 'allowed-tools:' field present
description: commands/setup.md declares no allowed-tools; invokes AskUserQuestion and Bash without restricting the tool surface
Vague quantifier 'briefly' gives no measurable output scope for the result report
suggested_fix: Replace 'briefly' with a concrete constraint, e.g. 'in one sentence'

evidence: line 10: 'overlap only when genuinely multi-category'; line 65: 'use it for a genuinely stuck slice'
description: Vague quantifier 'genuinely' used twice in section 4 with no measurable threshold for what constitutes a 'genuinely' multi-category or stuck situation
suggested_fix: Replace 'genuinely multi-category' with a concrete definition (e.g., 'matches two or more of the pack signals in §1–§3') and 'genuinely stuck' with an observable criterion (e.g., 'same failure class has recurred 2+ times')

description: transcript_path is extracted from hook-supplied JSON stdin and passed directly to python3 as sys.argv[1] then opened with open(path); no directory prefix validation constrains the read target
suggested_fix: After extracting tpath, verify it falls within an expected directory prefix (e.g., starts with $TMPDIR or $HOME/.claude) before passing it to the Python heredoc

description: FABLIZE_DATA environment variable overrides the ledger data root, allowing ledger files to be written to arbitrary directories controlled by an environment attacker
suggested_fix: Document the env var as intentional and validate that the resolved path stays within the user's home directory if untrusted environments are a concern

description: Measurement telemetry is silently appended to ~/.fablize/history/events.jsonl on every Stop hook invocation with no documented opt-out or user disclosure
suggested_fix: Add a disclosure note to SKILL.md install section and/or setup.sh indicating local session telemetry is collected; provide FABLIZE_NO_TELEMETRY opt-out

description: finish-the-work.sh declares itself a Stop hook and is referenced as a companion to gate_stop.py in that file's docstring, but is absent from the Stop section of hooks/hooks.json
suggested_fix: Add a second Stop hook entry for finish-the-work.sh in hooks/hooks.json, or confirm that setup.sh injects it via CLAUDE.md and document that in hooks/hooks.json comments
